title: Add content_warning metadata to MediaCMS
instructions: Add a new `content_warning` metadata field to MediaCMS media items. Carry it through the Django model, serializer/API surface, validation, and one visible product surface. Add targeted tests for save/read and validation behavior.
acceptance_commands: docker compose -f upstream/mediacms/docker-compose-dev.yaml -f docker/compose.benchmark.yaml up -d db redis migrations web celery_worker benchmark_runner benchmark_judge
docker compose -f upstream/mediacms/docker-compose-dev.yaml -f docker/compose.benchmark.yaml exec --env TESTING=True -T web pytest tests/api files/tests

docker compose -f upstream/mediacms/docker-compose-dev.yaml -f docker/compose.benchmark.yaml exec --env TESTING=True -T web python manage.py shell -c "from files.models import Media; from files.serializers import SingleMediaSerializer; field_names=[field.name for field in Media._meta.fields]; serializer_fields=SingleMediaSerializer().get_fields().keys(); assert 'content_warning' in field_names; assert 'content_warning' in serializer_fields"

extra.reference_solution_strategy: Modify Media model and serializer fields, update API-visible validation path, and add focused tests that prove save/read plus invalid-value rejection.
task_prompt_constraints: Do not modify benchmark harness files during task execution.
Keep changes localized to the MediaCMS app and its tests.
